page: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 architecture instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B12 processor. It features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading, providing two threads per core. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. This compute-heavy allocation is designed for workloads requiring substantial processing power relative to memory capacity. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks place the e2-highcpu-32 in the average tier for most multi-core and memory operations, while demonstrating strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing. The AMD EPYC processor includes hardware support for AVX, AES, and SHA-NI cryptographic extensions, which aids in processing encrypted workloads. In testing, the instance achieved a PassMark CPU score of 38354.69 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11360.0. Memory bandwidth tests recorded 82.87 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 51.96 GB/sec for large block write operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E2 family utilizes resource-dense hardware configurations to deliver reliable compute capacity without the premium associated with specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a cost-effective choice for CPU-bound tasks that do not demand massive memory footprints. This profile is well-suited for workloads such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 compute-focused instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B12 processor. It features 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores and is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 1.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 38354.69 and a static web serving throughput of 18.43 GB/sec. Lacking local NVMe drives or high-density RAM, this instance represents a cost-efficient option for processing-heavy workloads that do not require extensive memory footprints. It is highly suitable for tasks such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, and basic text processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-32 is an x86_64 instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B12 CPU. It delivers strong single-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-highcpu-32 features 32 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, offering a cost-efficient, compute-heavy profile powered by AMD EPYC processors.
tagline: A compute-focused x86_64 instance featuring thirty-two virtual cores and a dense one-to-one processor-to-memory ratio for processing-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7B12 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
32.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and multi-threaded decompression
